PrimaryIndustries: Flour milling; grain trading; lumber milling
Notes: Powered by St. Anthony Falls on the Mississippi; home to Pillsbury, Washburn-Crosby (later General Mills).

PrimaryIndustries: Rail hub; meatpacking/stockyards; steel; grain and commodities trading
Notes: Nation's central rail junction; Union Stock Yards (1865-1971); Chicago Board of Trade.

PrimaryIndustries: Finance/banking; port and international trade; garment manufacturing; publishing; corporate headquarters
Notes: Wall Street; the country's largest port and financial center throughout the period.
Established early-industrial base (textiles, shoes); major shipping port; shifted toward finance/education over the period.

PrimaryIndustries: Port and shipping; cotton and sugar exports; oil/petrochemicals (20th c.)
Notes: Historic Mississippi River port; relative importance declined vs. rail-connected interior cities after the Civil War.

PrimaryIndustries: Rail hub; banking/insurance; manufacturing and distribution
Notes: Largely destroyed in 1864; rebuilt as a railroad crossroads; Coca-Cola founded there in 1886.
Grew explosively in the Gold Rush (1849-); dominant West Coast financial center through the 1906 earthquake and after.

PrimaryIndustries: Timber/lumber; shipbuilding; port trade; aircraft manufacturing (20th c.)
Notes: Gateway port for the Klondike Gold Rush (1897-98); Boeing founded there in 1916.

PrimaryIndustries: Mining supply and ore smelting; rail hub; regional trade/distribution
Notes: Grew out of the 1858-59 Pike's Peak gold rush; became the Rocky Mountain region's rail and distribution center.

PrimaryIndustries: Cotton trading; banking; rail hub; oil industry (post-1930s)
Notes: Grew as a cotton and rail center; East Texas oil boom (from 1930) drove 20th-century growth into banking/insurance.
